textDisplay: Saw the movie last night after much anticipation. Unfortunately I was extremely disappointed. The Apostle Paul is the father of the faith of Christianity and he did not get his just rewards in this movie. James Faulkner was well casted for the part unfortunately the character of Paul was not developed in this movie because they didn't flash back to anything that he did that was unbelievably riveting as in the book of Acts. There are so many things that were downers about this movie. I definitely think the two other movies of the past based on the Apostle Paul's life were so much Superior
textOriginal: Saw the movie last night after much anticipation. Unfortunately I was extremely disappointed. The Apostle Paul is the father of the faith of Christianity and he did not get his just rewards in this movie. James Faulkner was well casted for the part unfortunately the character of Paul was not developed in this movie because they didn't flash back to anything that he did that was unbelievably riveting as in the book of Acts. There are so many things that were downers about this movie. I definitely think the two other movies of the past based on the Apostle Paul's life were so much Superior

textDisplay: For all those believers in Christ, this was a tear jerkier for me. Even though the movie was rated as good, the part of Paul touched my soul. It is amazing how knowing the Word and  the presence of the Spirit can touch our being by just imagining the suffering that Paul bared in the name of Christ. Last part of the movie did it... I could not hold my tears as I watched a man that I believe truly loved the LORD.

textDisplay: Paul is a servant of God. High up actually on his heavenly staff relating to this world. His thorn was what he did to the Kindred of Christ before he was struck blind and knocked into the dirt - a very diplomatic act upon the part of the Angel that smoothly performed that act. Saul deserved muuch worse. BUT he had promise, and OMG what promise had he! Paul saved more souls than any other on this planet. Acts Romans just read. he wrote many of the books that are included in the bible.

textDisplay: What do you mean "Jesus saw potential in this man"? As Paul said "Christ in you, the hope of glory" Colossians 1:27 and "it is no longer I who live, but Christ lives in me" Galatians 2:20 – people need to stop putting confidence in the flesh with all it's so called potential. Otherwise they will end up seeking out personal mentors and heroes – Jesus is the Way
textOriginal: What do you mean "Jesus saw potential in this man"? As Paul said "Christ in you, the hope of glory" Colossians 1:27 and "it is no longer I who live, but Christ lives in me" Galatians 2:20 – people need to stop putting confidence in the flesh with all it's so called potential. Otherwise they will end up seeking out personal mentors and heroes – Jesus is the Way

textDisplay: Luke wrote about the whole Way of the believers, not just about Paul. The New Testament does not give one account which Paul was doubting His belief. Paul wrote letters to the assembly to encourage them concerning the belief in Messiah he did not to create new doctrines. This is another twisted version of the Scriptures. The first problem is Paul never celebrated Easter. It was Passover, Unleavened Bread And First Fruits according to the Scriptures.
textOriginal: Luke wrote about the whole Way of the believers, not just about Paul. The New Testament does not give one account which Paul was doubting His belief. Paul wrote letters to the assembly to encourage them concerning the belief in Messiah he did not to create new doctrines. This is another twisted version of the Scriptures. The first problem is Paul never celebrated Easter. It was Passover, Unleavened Bread And First Fruits according to the Scriptures.

textDisplay: Paul also celebrated the Gospel. The reconciliation of all mankind with God.
textOriginal: Paul also celebrated the Gospel. The reconciliation of all mankind with God.

textDisplay: OlamYah exactly. Actually Peter was the first to go to the Gentiles not Paul. Just lies and deception formulated by the anti christ spirit

textDisplay: One Step Away Of course Peter was, he was with Jesus from the beginning. Paul was not. Paul was a well-educated Jewish man of the law who hated Christians, and killed them! Jesus spoke to him from Heaven with a blinding light! He said, “Paul, Paul, why do you persecute Me?” Paul realized he was wrong and he was blinded. He went to a Christian’s home to recover, and learn about Jesus. He became, as Peter did, fervent for Christ, spreading His Word wherever he went. And he, like Peter and all the disciples but one, was killed for his faith. They saw Jesus alive after He died on the Cross, would not deny what they saw, so were killed.
textOriginal: One Step Away Of course Peter was, he was with Jesus from the beginning. Paul was not. Paul was a well-educated Jewish man of the law who hated Christians, and killed them! Jesus spoke to him from Heaven with a blinding light! He said, “Paul, Paul, why do you persecute Me?” Paul realized he was wrong and he was blinded. He went to a Christian’s home to recover, and learn about Jesus. He became, as Peter did, fervent for Christ, spreading His Word wherever he went. And he, like Peter and all the disciples but one, was killed for his faith. They saw Jesus alive after He died on the Cross, would not deny what they saw, so were killed.

textDisplay: Saw this preview in a theatre today prior to "Samson" & was impressed.  Looks like a powerful portrayal of Paul.
textOriginal: Saw this preview in a theatre today prior to "Samson" & was impressed.  Looks like a powerful portrayal of Paul.

textDisplay: Yes!  There were some negatives, but mostly positives.  I mostly went to experience the full jawbone battle.  Was not disappointed by the majority of the scene, but was disappointed by a non-Bibliical incident at its end.  But there was also a non-Biblical scene not shown in the preview that was even better than the jawbone scene (similar to David confronting Goliath).
textOriginal: Yes!  There were some negatives, but mostly positives.  I mostly went to experience the full jawbone battle.  Was not disappointed by the majority of the scene, but was disappointed by a non-Bibliical incident at its end.  But there was also a non-Biblical scene not shown in the preview that was even better than the jawbone scene (similar to David confronting Goliath).

textDisplay: Another minor dissapointment was that they didn't make the most of Lindsay Wagner's cameo role.  I was hoping for some bionic humor about Samson's strength.  He had some humorous lines, but his mother's character was fairly bland/unemotional.  On the positive side though, the writer's wove some excellent Christian themes into the film about fidelity, repentance, & grace.
textOriginal: Another minor dissapointment was that they didn't make the most of Lindsay Wagner's cameo role.  I was hoping for some bionic humor about Samson's strength.  He had some humorous lines, but his mother's character was fairly bland/unemotional.  On the positive side though, the writer's wove some excellent Christian themes into the film about fidelity, repentance, & grace.
